title: strychnos rupicola
intro: Strychnos rupicola is a plant species belonging to the Strychnos genus, native to tropical Africa, and is known for its toxic yet medicinal properties. The plant contains a compound called strychnine, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism and snake bites. Medicinally, Strychnos rupicola has been used to treat fever, malaria, and various skin conditions, with health benefits including pain relief and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared as a powder or extract, often used in combination with other herbs to treat a range of health issues.
